purpose_string: Isolate whether start/end LTX guide strength near 0.7 improves a trackable in-place horse walk while keeping the prompt, seed, reference, workflow, frame count, and output settings identical.
positive_prompt_string: Locked-camera inverse skeletal fitting reference of the exact low-poly horse shown in the supplied canonical render. Preserve the same individual, geometry, proportions, materials, four limbs, rigid skeleton-readable silhouette, lighting, ground plane, framing, and background. Keep the complete body and all four hooves visible. The horse performs one anatomically correct four-beat lateral-sequence walk cycle in place at an even controller-friendly cadence. Hoof contacts occur separately in this camera-readable order: near hind, near fore, far hind, far fore, then repeat, with each contact approximately one quarter-cycle apart. Natural swing phases may overlap, but paired hooves never lift or land synchronously; at least two hooves support the body continuously. Every stance hoof stays planted flat on the ground until natural lift-off, then follows a clean forward swing and lands without sliding. Maintain fixed rigid long-bone segment lengths and bend only at anatomical joints. Every hoof remains a compact solid block with unchanged shape. Use stable body mass, believable subtle head, neck, spine, and pelvis motion, and no net translation. Create exactly one seamless cyclic action. The supplied base-pose image is both frame 0 and frame N-1; treat that pose as a moving pass-through phase, never a hold. Motion phase, body pose, root velocity, and all limb velocities close smoothly across the seam without a pause, duplicate beat, reversal, teleport, or snap.
negative_prompt_string: camera movement, pan, tilt, orbit, dolly, zoom, tracking shot, handheld shake, reframing, crop, scene cut, background drift, parallax, identity change, species change, altered proportions, extra limb, missing limb, fused limb, duplicated subject, simultaneous paired hoof strike, pacing, trotting, hopping, galloping, suspension phase, broken joint, dislocated joint, rubber leg, telescoping limb, changing segment length, curled distal limb, hooked distal limb, curled hoof, hooked hoof, hoof morphing, stretched hoof, toe elongation, toe drag, floating hoof, foot sliding, hoof skating, ground penetration, topology change, material change, flicker, motion smear, endpoint freeze, pause at seam, duplicated endpoint beat, motion reversal, text, watermark, logo
controlled_variable_string: LTXVAddGuide strength on both frame 0 and frame N-1
fixed_factors_array: worker endpoint and pinned workflow fingerprint
sampler, scheduler, model, latent size, and output codec settings
manual_acceptance_gates_array: exactly four persistent limbs and four persistent compact hooves
near-hind near-fore far-hind far-fore four-beat lateral contact order
at least two supporting hooves with stance hooves visibly planted
no distal-limb curl, hoof morph, segment-length change, skating, or toe drag
stable identity, topology, camera, background, and ground contact
continuous motion through the loop seam without endpoint pause or reversal
motion is readable enough for frame-by-frame skeletal fitting

conditioning_followup_object.next_isolated_variable_string: derived high-contrast limb-labelled canonical render at higher native resolution
conditioning_followup_object.safety_requirement_string: Derive labels from rig bone names and skin weights or a new Blender render; never paint semantic near/far limb labels by eye and never mutate the immutable canonical bundle.
conditioning_followup_object.reference_observation_string: The current 768x448 subject mask occupies 35.16 percent of image width and 67.86 percent of image height; the 512x320 workflow therefore leaves thin same-color distal limbs with limited pixel separation.

biomechanics_source_object.title_string: Temporal analysis of walk movements in the Grand Prix dressage test at the 1996 Olympic Games
biomechanics_source_object.supported_claim_string: The horse walk is a four-beat lateral sequence: hind hoof, ipsilateral fore hoof, contralateral hind hoof, contralateral fore hoof.
